title: Sudden Weakness in All Four Legs in Dogs: Emergency Triage Guide
meta_desc: Sudden weakness in all four legs (tetraparesis) is a high-priority veterinary emergency. Learn the causes and why you must seek immediate care.
content_html:
What is Sudden Weakness in All Four Legs?

Sudden weakness in all four limbs, medically referred to as tetraparesis, occurs when a dog loses the ability to coordinate or support weight on their legs. This condition can range from a wobbly, "drunken" gait to a complete inability to stand or move. It is typically caused by a disruption in the nervous system, specifically affecting the brain, the spinal cord in the neck area, or the peripheral nerves and muscles.

Is This a Veterinary Emergency?

Urgency Level: High. Yes, sudden weakness in all four legs is considered a major veterinary emergency. Because this symptom often indicates a serious condition—such as a herniated disc (IVDD), spinal trauma, tick paralysis, botulism, or even a stroke—immediate intervention is vital. Delaying treatment can lead to permanent paralysis or life-threatening respiratory failure if the underlying cause affects the muscles used for breathing.

Why a Photo or Video Helps Triage

When you contact an emergency vet, providing a short video of your dog's movement (if they are still able to move at all) or their resting posture can be a game-changer. Animals often experience an adrenaline surge at the clinic that can temporarily mask the severity of their weakness. A video captured in a home environment allows the veterinarian to see the exact nature of the neurological deficit, helping them differentiate between weakness, pain, or lack of coordination before they even begin a physical exam.

faqs.question: Can I wait until morning to see my regular vet?
faqs.answer: No. Sudden weakness involving all four limbs requires immediate evaluation at an emergency hospital to prevent irreversible damage.

faqs.question: How should I move my dog if they can't walk?
faqs.answer: Gently slide your dog onto a flat, firm surface like a piece of plywood or a thick, supportive blanket to act as a stretcher, keeping their spine as straight as possible.

faqs.question: What are common causes for sudden collapse in all legs?
faqs.answer: Common causes include Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD) in the neck, Fibrocartilaginous Embolism (FCE), tick paralysis, or acute polyradiculoneuritis.
